findings.title: --filter is not treated as literal text and can make dscli top fail
findings.impact: The option is documented as a substring filter, but the changed line interpolates the raw value into a shell grep command. Inputs that are valid text filters but invalid shell/grep syntax, such as a single quote or an unmatched bracket, make grep/the shell return an error and the command raises instead of producing a filtered list. Regex metacharacters such as '.' also match more workers than the requested literal text, so operators can get a wrong worker set.
findings.fix: Avoid shell/grep for this filter. Run pgrep without the operator filter using argv form, parse the worker lines, and apply `if filter_text in cmdline` or equivalent literal matching in Python.

findings.title: --min-rss excludes workers exactly at the requested threshold
findings.impact: The help text and PR contract say workers using at least MB should be shown, but the predicate uses `rss_mb > min_rss_mb`. A worker whose RSS equals `--min-rss` is incorrectly hidden, including `--min-rss 0` hiding readable zero-MiB results.
findings.fix: Change the predicate to `rss_mb >= min_rss_mb` so the boundary matches the documented inclusive threshold.
